doctrine: v11 LOCKED: a framework MAPPING is ADVISORY alignment, NOT a certification — SZL holds no third-party AI-governance certificate today. Λ=Conjecture 1 (advisory, never 'proven trust'/'proven compliant'). Each cell labelled IMPLEMENTED/PARTIAL/ROADMAP honestly; coverage() counts ONLY IMPLEMENTED; a framework reads 100% ONLY if every mapped control is IMPLEMENTED. Framework IDs CITED from official sources, not claimed as SZL's. Sovereign own-metal, 0 CDN, no fabricated numbers.
lambda: Λ = Conjecture 1 (advisory). 'Aligned' = 'this SZL control supports the framework requirement', NEVER 'certified' or 'proven compliant'. Deny-by-default posture; a signature/attestation is NOT proof of safety (GAP1 lesson).
disclaimer: Self-asserted ADVISORY alignment to recognized AI-governance frameworks. NOT a certification or conformity assessment. SZL holds no third-party AI-governance certificate as of this artifact's generation.

status_vocabulary.IMPLEMENTED: Shipping in the estate today; demonstrable.
status_vocabulary.PARTIAL: Real but incomplete (e.g. engine built, not yet production-wired).
status_vocabulary.ROADMAP: Designed/intended; NOT yet shipping. Never counted as covered.

deny_by_default_lambda_gate.name: Deny-by-default Λ-gate (advisory governance gate)
deny_by_default_lambda_gate.evidence: Λ = Conjecture 1 admission gate, deny-by-default; ALLOW/NOMINAL = 'passed SZL admission policy', never 'proven trust'. Live in pnt_resilience fused detector and the mesh route table.

measured_modeled_labeling.name: MEASURED vs MODELED vs SAMPLE labeling on every value
measured_modeled_labeling.evidence: Every emitted value carries a provenance label; PINN compute-bounds certificate labels each input MEASURED and each derived bound MODELED. Honest 'NOT MODELED' where physics/data is incomplete.

signed_dsse_provenance_rekor.name: Signed DSSE provenance (Ed25519) + Rekor transparency
signed_dsse_provenance_rekor.evidence: DSSE Ed25519 envelope pattern + FA-001 + cosign.pub anchor + Rekor transparency are designed and the hash CHAIN is real and verified; per the authoritative docs/compliance.md the cosign SIGNING in CI is PENDING (DSSE-PLACEHOLDER) — so honestly PARTIAL, not IMPLEMENTED, until signing lands.

overclaim_ledger_ci_guard.name: Overclaim ledger + CI guard (banned-claim tripwire)
overclaim_ledger_ci_guard.evidence: Banned-claim ledger (e.g. 'SLSA L3', 'proven trust') with a CI guard / tripwire T02 (measurabilityHonesty). Authoritative posture page enforces it.

clean_room.name: Clean-room engineering (cite-never-plagiarize)
clean_room.evidence: Bounds/physics re-derived clean-room from papers; kshana method cited (Apache-2.0, DOI) not copied. Provenance of method documented.

sovereign_own_metal.name: Sovereign own-metal deployment (no hyperscaler dependency)
sovereign_own_metal.evidence: 2 own GPUs, air-gap-capable, sovereign deployment model. No hyperscaler runtime dependency.

zero_cdn.name: Zero runtime CDN
zero_cdn.evidence: 0 runtime CDN; all assets served from sovereign own-metal.

behavioural_artifact_monitor.name: Behavioural artifact monitor (runtime, GAP1 — inward SDA/spoof fusion)
behavioural_artifact_monitor.evidence: GAP1 engine: turns the deny-by-default SDA/spoof-fusion detector inward onto our OWN build/deploy artifacts (anomaly on tarball size, egress, unexpected file injection) — the behavioural half the npm/TanStack SLSA-signed-malware story proved necessary. Built in this gap-closure cycle; PARTIAL until Forge wires it into the production deploy pipeline.

c2pa_content_credentials.name: C2PA Content Credentials on generated assets (GAP2)
c2pa_content_credentials.evidence: GAP2 engine: signed C2PA manifests (tool, edits, AI-or-not) on estate-generated assets, using existing cosign/DSSE identity, for EU AI Act Art. 50 marking. Built in this gap-closure cycle; PARTIAL until production assets emit it by default.

pqc_ml_dsa_signatures.name: Post-quantum (ML-DSA) signature option for DSSE signer (GAP6)
pqc_ml_dsa_signatures.evidence: GAP6: ML-DSA (FIPS 204) as an optional PQ signature alongside Ed25519, per the IETF Agent Trust Negotiation draft. NOT shipping — explicitly a roadmap option. Included here ONLY as ROADMAP so the crosswalk shows the honest gap; it is never counted as covered by coverage().

crosswalk.ref_name: Policies & processes to govern AI risk
crosswalk.rationale: Deny-by-default admission policy IS a documented risk-governance process.

crosswalk.ref_name: AI risk treatment; AI system operation & monitoring
crosswalk.rationale: Gate is an operational risk-treatment control on the AI system at runtime.
Supports Art.9 risk mgmt & Art.14 oversight, but no formal high-risk QMS yet.
Provenance labels make every value's epistemic status explicit and testable.

crosswalk.ref_name: AI system technical documentation; quality of data
crosswalk.rationale: Labels are technical-documentation evidence of data/derivation quality.
Labels support transparency & honest accuracy claims; not a full Art.13 deployer pack.
Provenance + transparency log support traceability; signing in CI still PENDING.

crosswalk.ref_name: Data provenance; recording of event logs
crosswalk.rationale: DSSE + Rekor are provenance & event-log evidence; PARTIAL until signer wired.
Rekor transparency log is tamper-evident record-keeping; signing PENDING.
CI guard structurally blocks overclaims — an accountability control.
Banned-claim ledger enforces policy; a failing guard is a corrective-action trigger.

crosswalk.ref_name: Transparency & provision of information to deployers
crosswalk.rationale: Prevents misleading capability claims; not the full Art.13 instruction set.
Cite-never-plagiarize controls IP/provenance risk of reused methods.
Documented provenance of third-party methods/data — a supplier/resource control.
Supports lawful, documented data/method provenance; not full Art.10 dataset governance.

crosswalk.ref_name: Resources to manage risk; supply-chain risk
crosswalk.rationale: No-hyperscaler posture removes a class of supply-chain/availability risk.
Own-metal is a documented, controlled computing-resource posture.
Air-gap-capable own-metal aids robustness/cyber posture; not a full Art.15 conformity test.
Removing runtime CDN removes a third-party tamper/availability dependency.
No third-party CDN supplier in the runtime path — a documented supplier control.
Reduces runtime attack surface; supports but does not satisfy Art.15 alone.
Runtime behavioural anomaly monitor on our own artifacts; built, not yet prod-wired.

crosswalk.ref_name: AI system operation & monitoring; event logs
crosswalk.rationale: Operational monitoring control producing event logs; PARTIAL pending deploy wiring.
Behavioural monitor feeds post-market monitoring & logs; not yet a formal PMM system.
Signed content manifests make generated outputs traceable AI-or-not.

crosswalk.ref_name: Data provenance; system docs & info for users
crosswalk.rationale: Content Credentials are provenance metadata informing users; PARTIAL pending default-on.

crosswalk.ref_name: Transparency: machine-readable marking of AI-generated content
crosswalk.rationale: C2PA is the named mechanism for Art.50 marking (enforceable 2 Aug 2026); built, not default-on.
PQ crypto agility is a designed risk-treatment option; NOT shipping yet (roadmap).
PQ signatures would future-proof log/provenance integrity; roadmap, not implemented.
PQ readiness strengthens long-term cybersecurity posture; explicitly roadmap.

coverage.interpretation: pct_implemented = fraction of MAPPED cells our IMPLEMENTED controls currently support. It is an honest self-assessment of alignment coverage, NOT third-party-certified coverage and NOT a claim that the framework as a whole is satisfied. SZL holds no AI-governance certificate today; PARTIAL/ROADMAP cells are the honest gap to close.
